children.heading: Administration
children.label: 1 Administration
children.text: The term “administration”, when used with respect to an eligible agency or eligible recipient, means activities necessary for the proper and efficient performance of the eligible agency or eligible recipient’s duties under this chapter, including the supervision of such activities. Such term does not include curriculum development activities, personnel development, or research activities.

children.heading: All aspects of an industry
children.label: 2 All aspects of an industry
children.text: The term “all aspects of an industry” means strong experience in, and comprehensive understanding of, the industry that the individual is preparing to enter.

children.heading: Area career and technical education school
children.label: 3 Area career and technical education school
A
children.text: a specialized public secondary school used exclusively or principally for the provision of career and technical education to individuals who are available for study in preparation for entering the labor market;

children.label: B
children.text: the department of a public secondary school exclusively or principally used for providing career and technical education in not fewer than 3 different fields that are available to all students, especially in high-skill, high-wage, or in-demand industry sectors or occupations;

children.label: C
children.text: a public or nonprofit technical institution or career and technical education school used exclusively or principally for the provision of career and technical education to individuals who have completed or left secondary school and who are available for study in preparation for entering the labor market, if the institution or school admits, as regular students, individuals who have completed secondary school and individuals who have left secondary school; or

children.label: D
children.text: the department or division of an institution of higher education, that operates under the policies of the eligible agency and that provides career and technical education in not fewer than 3 different occupational fields leading to immediate employment but not necessarily leading to a baccalaureate degree, if the department or division admits, as regular students, both individuals who have completed secondary school and individuals who have left secondary school.

children.label: i
children.text: a secondary institution and a postsecondary educational institution; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a subbaccalaureate degree granting postsecondary educational institution and a baccalaureate degree granting postsecondary educational institution; and

children.label: i
children.text: designed to provide students with a nonduplicative sequence of progressive achievement leading to technical skill proficiency, a credential, a certificate, or a degree; and

children.label: ii
children.text: linked through credit transfer agreements between the 2 institutions described in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (A) (as the case may be).

children.label: i
children.text: provides individuals with rigorous academic content and relevant technical knowledge and skills needed to prepare for further education and careers in current or emerging professions, which may include high-skill, high-wage, or in-demand industry sectors or occupations, which shall be, at the secondary level, aligned with the challenging State academic standards adopted by a State under ;section 6311(b)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: provides technical skill proficiency or a recognized postsecondary credential, which may include an industry-recognized credential, a certificate, or an associate degree; and

children.label: iii
children.text: may include prerequisite courses (other than a remedial course) that meet the requirements of this subparagraph;

children.label: B
children.text: include competency-based, work-based, or other applied learning that supports the development of academic knowledge, higher-order reasoning and problem-solving skills, work attitudes, employability skills, technical skills, and occupation-specific skills, and knowledge of all aspects of an industry, including entrepreneurship, of an individual;

children.label: C
children.text: to the extent practicable, coordinate between secondary and postsecondary education programs through programs of study, which may include coordination through articulation agreements, early college high school programs, dual or concurrent enrollment program opportunities, or other credit transfer agreements that provide postsecondary credit or advanced standing; and

children.label: D
children.text: may include career exploration at the high school level or as early as the middle grades (as such term is defined in ).section 7801 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “career and technical student organization” means an organization for individuals enrolled in a career and technical education program that engages in career and technical education activities as an integral part of the instructional program.

children.heading: State and national units
children.label: B State and national units
children.text: An organization described in subparagraph (A) may have State and national units that aggregate the work and purposes of instruction in career and technical education at the local level.

children.label: A
children.text: provides access for students (and, as appropriate, parents and out-of-school youth) to information regarding career awareness exploration opportunities and planning with respect to an individual’s occupational and academic future;

children.label: B
children.text: provides information to students (and, as appropriate, parents and out-of-school youth) with respect to career options, financial aid, job training, secondary and postsecondary options (including associate and baccalaureate degree programs), dual or concurrent enrollment programs, work-based learning opportunities, early college high schools, financial literacy, and support services, as appropriate; and

children.label: C
children.text: may provide assistance for special populations with respect to direct support services that enable students to persist in and complete career and technical education, programs of study, or career pathways.

children.heading: Career pathways
children.label: 8 Career pathways
children.text: The term “career pathways” has the meaning given the term in .section 3102 of title 29

children.heading: Charter school
children.label: 9 Charter school
children.text: The term “charter school” has the meaning given the term in .section 7221i of this title

children.label: A
children.text: shall be planned and supervised by the school and employer so that each contributes to the education and employability of the individual; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include an arrangement in which work periods and school attendance may be on alternate half days, full days, weeks, or other periods of time in fulfilling the cooperative program.

children.heading: Credit transfer agreement
children.label: 11 Credit transfer agreement
children.text: The term “credit transfer agreement” means a formal agreement, such as an articulation agreement, among and between secondary and postsecondary education institutions or systems that grant students transcripted postsecondary credit, which may include credit granted to students in dual or concurrent enrollment programs or early college high school, dual credit, articulated credit, and credit granted on the basis of performance on technical or academic assessments.

children.label: A
children.text: at the secondary school level, a student served by an eligible recipient who has completed at least 2 courses in a single career and technical education program or program of study; and

children.label: i
children.text: earned at least 12 credits within a career and technical education program or program of study; or

children.label: ii
children.text: completed such a program if the program encompasses fewer than 12 credits or the equivalent in total.

children.heading: CTE participant
children.label: 13 CTE participant
children.text: The term “CTE participant” means an individual who completes not less than one course in a career and technical education program or program of study of an eligible recipient.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 14 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the Institute of Education Sciences.

children.heading: Dual or concurrent enrollment program
children.label: 15 Dual or concurrent enrollment program
children.text: The term “dual or concurrent enrollment program” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Early college high school
children.label: 16 Early college high school
children.text: The term “early college high school” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Educational service agency
children.label: 17 Educational service agency
children.text: The term “educational service agency” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Eligible agency
children.label: 18 Eligible agency
children.text: The term “eligible agency” means a State board designated or created consistent with State law as the sole State agency responsible for the administration of career and technical education in the State or for the supervision of the administration of career and technical education in the State.

children.label: i
children.text: A local educational agency or a consortium of such agencies.

children.label: ii
children.text: An educational service agency serving secondary school students.

children.label: iii
children.text: An area career and technical education school or a consortium of such schools.

children.label: iv
children.text: An Indian Tribe, Tribal organization, or Tribal educational agency.

children.label: v
children.text: An institution of higher education whose most common degree awarded is an associate degree, or a consortium of such institutions.

children.label: vi
children.text: An institution of higher education whose most common degree awarded is a bachelor’s or higher degree, or a consortium of such institutions.

children.label: vii
children.text: A State educational agency.

children.label: B
children.text: One or more business or industry representative partners, which may include representatives of local or regional businesses or industries, including industry or sector partnerships in the local area, local workforce development boards, or labor organizations.

children.label: i
children.text: parents and students;

children.label: ii
children.text: representatives of local agencies serving out-of-school youth, homeless children and youth, and at-risk youth (as defined in );section 6472 of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: representatives of Indian tribes and Tribal organizations, where applicable;

children.label: iv
children.text: representatives of minority-serving institutions (as described in paragraphs (1) through (7) of ), where applicable;section 1067q(a) of this title

children.label: v
children.text: representatives of special populations;

children.label: vi
children.text: representatives of adult career and technical education providers; or

children.label: vii
children.text: other relevant community stakeholders.

children.label: A
children.text: a consortium of 2 or more of the entities described in subparagraphs (B) through (F);

children.label: B
children.text: a public or nonprofit private institution of higher education that offers and will use funds provided under this subchapter in support of career and technical education courses that lead to technical skill proficiency or a recognized postsecondary credential, including an industry-recognized credential, a certificate, or an associate degree, except that, for the purpose of , the term “recognized postsecondary credential” as used in this subparagraph shall not include a baccalaureate degree;section 2352 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: a local educational agency providing education at the postsecondary level;

children.label: D
children.text: an area career and technical education school providing education at the postsecondary level;

children.label: E
children.text: an Indian Tribe, Tribal organization, or Tribal education agency that operates a school or may be present in the State;

children.label: F
children.text: a postsecondary educational institution controlled by the Bureau of Indian Education or operated by or on behalf of any Indian Tribe that is eligible to contract with the Secretary of the Interior for the administration of programs under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.) or the Act of ( et seq.);25 U.S.C. 530125 U.S.C. 5342April 16, 1934

children.label: G
children.text: a tribally controlled college or university; or

children.label: H
children.text: an educational service agency.

children.label: A
children.text: a local educational agency (including a public charter school that operates as a local educational agency), an area career and technical education school, an educational service agency, an Indian Tribe, Tribal organization, or Tribal educational agency or a consortium, eligible to receive assistance under ; orsection 2351 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: an eligible institution or consortium of eligible institutions eligible to receive assistance under .section 2352 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a secondary school student who is an English learner, as defined in ; orsection 7801 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: whose native language is a language other than English; or

children.label: ii
children.text: who lives in a family environment or community in which a language other than English is the dominant language.

children.heading: Evidence-based
children.label: 23 Evidence-based
children.text: The term “evidence-based” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801(21)(A) of this title

children.heading: Governor
children.label: 24 Governor
children.text: The term “Governor” means the chief executive officer of a State.

children.heading: High school
children.label: 25 High school
children.text: The term “high school” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: In-demand industry sector or occupation
children.label: 26 In-demand industry sector or occupation
children.text: The term “in-demand industry sector or occupation” has the meaning given the term in .section 3102 of title 29

children.heading: Indian; Indian Tribe
children.label: 27 Indian; Indian Tribe
children.text: The terms “Indian” and “Indian Tribe” have the meanings given the terms “Indian” and “Indian tribe”, respectively, in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ().25 U.S.C. 5304

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “individual with a disability” means an individual with any disability (as defined in ).section 12102 of title 42

children.heading: Individuals with disabilities
children.label: B Individuals with disabilities
children.text: The term “individuals with disabilities” means more than 1 individual with a disability.

children.heading: Industry or sector partnership
children.label: 29 Industry or sector partnership
children.text: The term “industry or sector partnership” has the meaning given the term in .section 3102 of title 29

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 30 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given the term in .section 1001 of this title

children.heading: Local educational agency
children.label: 31 Local educational agency
children.text: The term “local educational agency” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Local workforce development board
children.label: 32 Local workforce development board
children.text: The term “local workforce development board” means a local workforce development board established under .section 3122 of title 29

children.heading: Non-traditional fields
children.label: 33 Non-traditional fields
children.text: The term “non-traditional fields” means occupations or fields of work, such as careers in computer science, technology, and other current and emerging high skill occupations, for which individuals from one gender comprise less than 25 percent of the individuals employed in each such occupation or field of work.

children.heading: Outlying area
children.label: 34 Outlying area
children.text: The term “outlying area” means the United States Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and the Republic of Palau.

children.heading: Out-of-school youth
children.label: 35 Out-of-school youth
children.text: The term “out-of-school youth” has the meaning given the term in .section 3102 of title 29

children.label: A
children.text: an individual who is a displaced homemaker, as defined in ; orsection 3102 of title 29

children.label: I
children.text: has worked primarily without remuneration to care for a home and family, and for that reason has diminished marketable skills; or

children.label: II
children.text: is a parent whose youngest dependent child will become ineligible to receive assistance under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act ( et seq.) not later than 2 years after the date on which the parent applies for assistance under such title; and42 U.S.C. 601

children.label: ii
children.text: is unemployed or underemployed and is experiencing difficulty in obtaining or upgrading employment.

children.heading: Paraprofessional
children.label: 37 Paraprofessional
children.text: The term “paraprofessional” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: in which a commitment is made to pay for improved outcomes that result in increased public value and social benefit to students and the public sector, such as improved student outcomes as evidenced by the indicators of performance described in and direct cost savings or cost avoidance to the public sector; andsection 2323(b)(2) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: a feasibility study on the initiative describing how the proposed intervention is based on evidence of effectiveness;

children.label: II
children.text: a rigorous, third-party evaluation that uses experimental or quasi-experimental design or other research methodologies that allow for the strongest possible causal inferences to determine whether the initiative has met its proposed outcomes;

children.label: III
children.text: an annual, publicly available report on the progress of the initiative; and

children.label: IV
children.text: a requirement that payments are made to the recipient of a grant, contract, or cooperative agreement only when agreed upon outcomes are achieved, except that the entity may make payments to the third party conducting the evaluation described in subclause (II).

children.label: i
children.text: reduces the special education or related services that a student would otherwise receive under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act [ et seq.]; or20 U.S.C. 1400

children.label: ii
children.text: otherwise reduces the rights of a student or the obligations of an entity under the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act, the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 ( et seq.), the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 ( et seq.), or any other law.29 U.S.C. 70142 U.S.C. 12101

children.label: A
children.text: an institution of higher education that provides not less than a 2-year program of instruction that is acceptable for credit toward a bachelor’s degree;

children.label: B
children.text: a tribally controlled college or university; or

children.label: C
children.text: a nonprofit educational institution offering certificate or other skilled training programs at the postsecondary level.

children.label: A
children.text: are an integral part of eligible agency, eligible recipient, institution, or school strategies for providing educators (including teachers, principals, other school leaders, administrators, specialized instructional support personnel, career guidance and academic counselors, and paraprofessionals) with the knowledge and skills necessary to enable students to succeed in career and technical education, to meet challenging State academic standards under , or to achieve academic skills at the postsecondary level; andsection 6311(b)(1) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: knowledge of the academic and technical subjects;

children.label: II
children.text: understanding of how students learn; and

children.label: III
children.text: ability to analyze student work and achievement from multiple sources, including how to adjust instructional strategies, assessments, and materials based on such analysis;

children.label: ii
children.text: are an integral part of eligible recipients’ improvement plans;

children.label: iii
children.text: allow personalized plans for each educator to address the educator’s specific needs identified in observation or other feedback;

children.label: iv
children.text: support the recruitment, hiring, and training of effective educators, including educators who became certified through State and local alternative routes to certification;

children.label: I
children.text: effective instructional strategies that are evidence-based; and

children.label: II
children.text: strategies for improving student academic and technical achievement or substantially increasing the knowledge and teaching skills of educators;

children.label: vi
children.text: are developed with extensive participation of educators, parents, students, and representatives of Indian Tribes (as applicable), of schools and institutions served under this chapter;

children.label: vii
children.text: are designed to give educators of students who are English learners in career and technical education programs or programs of study the knowledge and skills to provide instruction and appropriate language and academic support services to those students, including the appropriate use of curricula and assessments;

children.label: viii
children.text: as a whole, are regularly evaluated for their impact on increased educator effectiveness and improved student academic and technical achievement, with the findings of the evaluations used to improve the quality of professional development;

children.label: ix
children.text: are designed to give educators of individuals with disabilities in career and technical education programs or programs of study the knowledge and skills to provide instruction and academic support services to those individuals, including positive behavioral interventions and supports, multi-tier system of supports, and use of accommodations;

children.label: x
children.text: include instruction in the use of data and assessments to inform and instruct classroom practice;

children.label: xi
children.text: include instruction in ways that educators may work more effectively with parents and families;

children.label: xii
children.text: provide follow-up training to educators who have participated in activities described in this paragraph that are designed to ensure that the knowledge and skills learned by the educators are implemented in the classroom;

children.label: xiii
children.text: promote the integration of academic knowledge and skills and relevant technical knowledge and skills, including programming jointly delivered to academic and career and technical education teachers; or

children.label: xiv
children.text: increase the ability of educators providing career and technical education instruction to stay current with industry standards.

children.label: A
children.text: incorporates challenging State academic standards, including those adopted by a State under ;section 6311(b)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: addresses both academic and technical knowledge and skills, including employability skills;

children.label: C
children.text: is aligned with the needs of industries in the economy of the State, region, Tribal community, or local area;

children.label: D
children.text: progresses in specificity (beginning with all aspects of an industry or career cluster and leading to more occupation-specific instruction);

children.label: E
children.text: has multiple entry and exit points that incorporate credentialing; and

children.label: F
children.text: culminates in the attainment of a recognized postsecondary credential.

children.label: A
children.text: connecting employers to classrooms;

children.label: B
children.text: assisting in the design and implementation of career and technical education programs and programs of study;

children.label: C
children.text: delivering professional development;

children.label: D
children.text: connecting students to internships and other work-based learning opportunities; and

children.label: E
children.text: developing personalized student supports.

children.heading: Recognized postsecondary credential
children.label: 43 Recognized postsecondary credential
children.text: The term “recognized postsecondary credential” has the meaning given the term in .section 3102 of title 29

children.heading: Secondary school
children.label: 44 Secondary school
children.text: The term “secondary school” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 45 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Education.

children.heading: Specialized instructional support personnel
children.label: 46 Specialized instructional support personnel
children.text: The term “specialized instructional support personnel” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Specialized instructional support services
children.label: 47 Specialized instructional support services
children.text: The term “specialized instructional support services” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: individuals with disabilities;

children.label: B
children.text: individuals from economically disadvantaged families, including low-income youth and adults;

children.label: C
children.text: individuals preparing for non-traditional fields;

children.label: D
children.text: single parents, including single pregnant women;

children.label: E
children.text: out-of-workforce individuals;

children.label: F
children.text: English learners;

children.label: G
children.text: homeless individuals described in ;section 11434a of title 42

children.label: H
children.text: youth who are in, or have aged out of, the foster care system; and

children.label: i
children.text: is a member of the armed forces (as such term is defined in ); andsection 101(a)(4) of title 10

children.label: ii
children.text: is on active duty (as such term is defined in section 101(d)(1) of such title).

children.heading: State
children.label: 49 State
children.text: The term “State”, unless otherwise specified, means each of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and each outlying area.

children.heading: Support services
children.label: 50 Support services
children.text: The term “support services” means services related to curriculum modification, equipment modification, classroom modification, supportive personnel (including paraprofessionals and specialized instructional support personnel), and instructional aids and devices.

children.heading: Tribally controlled college or university
children.label: 51 Tribally controlled college or university
children.text: The term “tribally controlled college or university” has the meaning given the term in .section 1801(a) of title 25

children.heading: Tribally controlled postsecondary career and technical institution
children.label: 52 Tribally controlled postsecondary career and technical institution
A
children.text: is formally controlled, or has been formally sanctioned or chartered, by the governing body of an Indian Tribe or Indian Tribes;

children.label: B
children.text: offers a technical degree or certificate granting program;

children.label: C
children.text: is governed by a board of directors or trustees, a majority of whom are Indians;

children.label: D
children.text: demonstrates adherence to stated goals, a philosophy, or a plan of operation, that fosters individual Indian economic and self-sufficiency opportunity, including programs that are appropriate to stated Tribal goals of developing individual entrepreneurships and self-sustaining economic infrastructures on reservations or tribal lands;

children.label: E
children.text: has been in operation for at least 3 years;

children.label: F
children.text: holds accreditation with or is a candidate for accreditation by a nationally recognized accrediting authority for postsecondary career and technical education; and

children.label: G
children.text: enrolls the full-time equivalent of not less than 100 students, of whom a majority are Indians.

children.heading: Tribal organization
children.label: 53 Tribal organization
children.text: The term “Tribal organization” has the meaning given the term “tribal organization” in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ().25 U.S.C. 5304

children.heading: Universal design for learning
children.label: 54 Universal design for learning
children.text: The term “universal design for learning” has the meaning given the term in .section 7801 of this title

children.heading: Work-based learning
children.label: 55 Work-based learning
children.text: The term “work-based learning” means sustained interactions with industry or community professionals in real workplace settings, to the extent practicable, or simulated environments at an educational institution that foster in-depth, firsthand engagement with the tasks required in a given career field, that are aligned to curriculum and instruction.
